what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard message that announces a new microphone input button in the chat UI, explains how to use it, notes that it relies on browser built-in speech recognition, and asks recipients to test it and confirm whether auto-reload-on-deploy refreshed their screen.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type:message, source:claude_code, channel:dragnet-development, state:sent, and identical created/updated timestamps
Body describes a microphone button placed next to the image icon in the chat
Listening state is indicated by a pulsing red UI state
Speech recognition is implemented via the browser's built-in API
The message frames this as the first real test of auto-reload-on-deploy
The author explicitly asks for feedback on both the mic and the auto-reload behavior
standouts: First real test of auto-reload-on-deploy is tied directly to a user-facing feature launch
Microphone feature shipped using browser-native speech recognition rather than a backend service
Message is a direct call for user feedback on two separate things
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and markdown body
Includes a history array documenting the state transition to sent
State is marked sent and links are empty
concerns: Title is truncated and ends abruptly at 'th'
No code symbols are present, so the claim is not verifiable from this file alone
Auto-reload success is asserted but not evidenced here
Browser speech recognition behavior varies by browser and permission state, which is not addressed
notes: A development-channel notice announcing the new chat microphone feature and using its rollout to validate the auto-reload-on-deploy pipeline.
questions: Which chat application or product does this refer to?
What is the 'image icon' and where is it defined?
Did the auto-reload-on-deploy actually trigger for users?
What is the browser support matrix for the built-in speech recognition API?
What is the dragnet-development project or channel context?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it announces the microphone feature and asks for feedback on auto-reload-on-deploy.
intention_signals: The message is trying to inform the team that microphone input is live, guide them through using it, and simultaneously validate the auto-reload deployment mechanism by observing their experience.
novelty: It treats a routine feature announcement as a live test of the deployment pipeline, explicitly linking UX feedback to infrastructure validation.
contribution: It preserves a timestamped, human-readable record of when the microphone feature was introduced and how the auto-reload-on-deploy behavior was meant to be verified, which helps trace feature and deployment history.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item-e9169c05 in the dragnet-development channel announces a new chat microphone button using browser built-in speech recognition and asks users to confirm whether auto-reload-on-deploy refreshed their screen.
intention_for_embedding: To notify the team that microphone input is available and to collect feedback on both the feature and the auto-reload deployment behavior.
